profile.name: Saeed Amiri
profile.headline: ML & MLOps Engineer | Data Scientist | Physicist (Dr. rer. nat.)

labels.noSkillsYet: Add your key skills to unlock evidence popups.

bio:
Physicist (PhD) and Machine Learning Engineer with extensive experience in large‑scale data analysis, scientific simulation, and automated workflow design. 
 I build robust ML and MLOps systems, design reproducible pipelines, and work comfortably with high‑dimensional, fast‑moving datasets. My focus is on translating complex analytical workflows into scalable, reliable services that operate in real‑time environments. 
I bridge the gap between research prototypes and production systems, ensuring clarity, maintainability, and operational stability. Driven by analytical rigor and system thinking, I enjoy developing solutions that support data‑driven decision‑making in technically demanding domains.

experience.summary: Weather prediction pipeline from model development and reproducibility to deployment and monitoring.
Anomaly detection in automotive CAN bus data with 100M+ records and extreme class imbalance.

experience.org: University of Bremen, Hybrid Materials Interfaces Group
experience.summary: Built scalable simulation and data-processing workflows for large HPC datasets and faster experimentation cycles.
Modeled atomic-scale friction systems on HLRN HPC clusters with strong gains in runtime, memory use, and reliability.
Managed payroll, financial operations, and tax compliance while improving cost tracking and forecasting.

trainings.summary: Docker, Kubernetes, API security, Airflow, Prefect, MLflow, DVC, Dagshub; completed 5-day AWS modules (Cloud Practitioner and Solutions Architect) and AWS MLOps; experiment tracking, orchestration, CI/CD, model serving with BentoML, and monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana.
Classification, regression, clustering, dimensionality reduction, and advanced ML; feature engineering, pipelines, deep learning (Dense NN, CNN, computer vision), time series, transformers, and LLMs.

education.role: Dr. rer. nat. in Theoretical Physics (Material Science)
education.summary: Dissertation on friction under active control in systems with tailored degrees of freedom; grade: magna cum laude.
Dissertation on band structure and quantum transmission through phosphorene with impurities and defects (DFT study of 2D materials).
